近世には「{{jalink|牛頭天王社}}」と称していたが、{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後に『延喜式』に従って「栗栖神社」に改称し、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}41年（1908年）4月15日に更に現社名に改称した。「{{jalink|栗栖|Q136002285}}」は現鎮座地一帯の旧称であろうとされる。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻治命（うましまぢのみこと）}} * {{jalink|品陀和気命}} 近世には「牛頭天王社」の社名が示すように{{jalink|牛頭天王|Q11570247}}を{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}としていたが、{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}20年（1735年）の『{{jalink|河内志}}』以来式内「栗栖神社」に比定され、「栗栖」は現鎮座地一帯を{{jalink|本貫|Q846706}}とした古代{{jalink|氏族|Q211503}}、{{jalink|栗栖連}}氏に因むものと考えられ、その祭神も『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』に「栗栖連。同じき神（{{jalink|神饒速日命}}）の子、于摩志摩治命（うましまぢのみこと）の後也」（河内国{{jalink|神別|Q11588768}}栗栖連条）とある栗栖連の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}、于摩志摩治命（宇麻志麻治命）とされたために、明治維新後に宇麻志麻治命に改めた。また、品陀和気命は明治5年に{{jalink|合祀|Q11412524}}された旧木戸村の氏神である八幡社の祭神である。 === 歴史 === 鎮座地は{{jalink|長瀬川 (大阪府)|Q11653384|長瀬川}}（旧{{jalink|大和川|Q9001527}}）に沿い、周囲よりはやや高い土地で{{jalink|弥生時代|Q207858}}から集落があったと考えられ『式内社調査報告』。、創祀年代は不詳であるものの、八尾市跡部には{{jalink|物部守屋|Q2424736}}の阿都の{{jalink|別業|Q11396814}}（『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』{{jalink|用明天皇|Q349281}}2年（587年）4月丙午（2日）条）が営まれたとされるように、八尾市一帯は{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}及びその同族に深く関係した地であるため、物部氏の同族でこの地を本貫とした栗栖連が祖神を祀ったのに創まり、その時期は{{jalink|古墳時代|Q459939}}に遡るとされる。{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}4年（{{jalink|862年|Q24993}}）4月12日に正六位上から従五位下に昇叙され、同年11月11日に{{jalink|官社}}とされ（『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）{{efn|『三代実録』{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}7年12月8日（884年）条にも正六位上から従五位下に昇叙された記事があるが、貞観の官社列格が確実であり（次注参照）、かつ列格の要件は通例従五位下であることから、この記事は誤りであるとされる。}}、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|延喜の制}}で{{jalink|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|Q11064932|官幣小社}}に列した{{efn|{{jalink|金剛寺 (河内長野市)|Q11646520|金剛寺本}}『延喜式神名帳』の「栗栖神社」には「貞栗以下加」との注記があり、これは貞観13年（871年）に完成した『{{jalink|貞観式|Q11634547}}』において初めて「栗栖神社」が「神名帳」に加載されたということである。ちなみに「栗以下」は栗栖神社と、加津良・{{jalink|仲村神社||中村}}の2神社を指す。}}。 中世以降の詳細は判っていないが、鎮座地一帯は{{jalink|八尾荘}}と呼ばれ、当神社は史料に欠くもののその中心的な神社として崇められたと思われる。{{jalink|南北朝時代 (日本)|Q264012|南北朝時代}}には当神社から西方200ｍにある{{jalink|常光寺 (八尾市)|Q25248064|常光寺}}の{{jalink|僧|Q193944}}顕幸（'''八尾別当'''と称した）が{{jalink|寺}}を中心に{{jalink|八尾城|Q11390786}}{{efn|八尾城のあった場所ははっきりせず、ここと八尾座（西郷の約2kmほど南方向、現在の南本町9丁目あたり）の2説がある。}}を構えて{{jalink|南朝 (日本)|Q3001082|南朝方}}に与したが、{{jalink|湊川の戦い|Q114620}}後{{jalink|北朝 (日本)|Q1201819|北朝方}}が城を入手したため、{{jalink|延元|Q1194159}}2年（{{jalink|1337年|Q6039}}）に攻め寄せた南朝方の兵火に遇っている。当時の当神社は常光寺の{{jalink|鎮守}}とされた可能性もあり、常光寺とともに八尾城の一郭に組み込まれていたと推定されるため、この兵火で罹災したと思われる。また、八尾荘は{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}の末期にも河内国{{jalink|守護|Q1141023}}の{{jalink|畠山氏|Q662881}}の内紛のために戦乱の地となっており、この際にも罹災したと思われる。 近世には牛頭天王社として'''天徳寺'''を{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}とする西郷村の氏神とされたが、明治初めの{{jalink|神仏判然令}}を受けて天徳寺を廃寺にして社名を「栗栖神社」に改め、明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）4月に木戸村の八幡社を合祀、西郷・木戸両村の氏神として村社に列した。明治41年4月15日に萱振村（現萱振町）の{{jalink|加津良神社}}と八尾中野（現西山本町）の八阪神社を合祀して現社名に改称したが、戦後に加津良、八阪の両神社は復祀している。 === 社殿 === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|右（北）から本殿、幣殿、拝殿]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|八尾城址の碑（左）と菅原神社（右）]] {{jalink|本殿|Q2044994}}は{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}、明治中頃の造替。{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}は梁間1間桁行3間の{{jalink|切妻造|Q13308575}}{{jalink|妻入}}、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}13年（1924年）の建築で{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}に接続する。拝殿は桁行5間梁間3間の{{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}}{{jalink|平入|Q3136194}}、{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}12年（1841年）の建築。幣殿と拝殿は{{jalink|瓦葺}}。 その他、神輿舎、社務所がある。 === 境内 === 境内社に'''鶯神社'''、'''菅原神社'''の2社がある。また本殿の東側、菅原神社の横に{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}6年（1931年）に建碑された八尾城址の{{jalink|石碑|Q178743}}がある。 === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|近鉄大阪線|Q1052545}}{{jalink|近鉄八尾駅|Q6414378|八尾駅}}から西へ約5分 === 関連項目 === * 初日山{{jalink|常光寺 (八尾市)|Q25248064|常光寺}}（{{jalink|八尾地蔵尊}}） * {{jalink|八尾天満宮}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ 公式] * {{jalink|八尾妙法寺}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * 八尾山{{jalink|智崇院}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html 公式] * {{jalink|天王神社|Q11443028}} * {{jalink|八尾 (八尾市)|Q11390778}} * {{jalink|矢作神社 (八尾市)|Q11583922}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * 式内社研究會編 『式内社調査報告』第4巻河内国、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、昭和54年 {{shinto-stub}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''八尾神社'''（やおじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|若江郡|Q11616589}}の「'''栗栖神社'''（くるす{{mdash}}）」に比定され（{{jalink|式内社}}）、{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284|旧村社}}。旧{{jalink|若江郡|Q11616589}}{{jalink|西郷村 (大阪府若江郡)||西郷}}、{{jalink|木戸村 (大阪府若江郡)||木戸}}両村（現八尾市{{jalink|本町 (八尾市)|Q11520678|本町}}5丁目の北側、本町6・7丁目、東本町3丁目あたり）の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}である{{efn|ちなみに神社の旧住所表記は 八尾市大字西郷1064番地で、境内東には西郷地区の自治会館がある。}}。 === 社名 === (河内長野市)|Q11646520|金剛寺本}}『延喜式神名帳』の「栗栖神社」には「貞栗以下加」との注記があり、これは貞観13年（871年）に完成した『{{jalink|貞観式|Q11634547}}』において初めて「栗栖神社」が「神名帳」に加載されたということである。ちなみに「栗以下」は栗栖神社と、加津良・{{jalink|仲村神社||中村}}の2神社を指す。}}。 中世以降の詳細は判っていないが、鎮座地一帯は{{jalink|八尾荘}}と呼ばれ、当神社は史料に欠くもののその中心的な神社として崇められたと思われる。{{jalink|南北朝時代 (日本)|Q264012|南北朝時代}}には当神社から西方200ｍにある{{jalink|常光寺 (八尾市)|Q25248064|常光寺}}の{{jalink|僧|Q193944}}顕幸（'''八尾別当'''と称した）が{{jalink|寺}}を中心に{{jalink|八尾城|Q11390786}}{{efn|八尾城のあった場所ははっきりせず、ここと八尾座（西郷の約2kmほど南方向、現在の南本町9丁目あたり）の2説がある。}}を構えて{{jalink|南朝 (日本)|Q3001082|南朝方}}に与したが、{{jalink|湊川の戦い|Q114620}}後{{jalink|北朝 (日本)|Q1201819|北朝方}}が城を入手したため、{{jalink|延元|Q1194159}}2年（{{jalink|1337年|Q6039}}）に攻め寄せた南朝方の兵火に遇っている。当時の当神社は常光寺の{{jalink|鎮守}}とされた可能性もあり、常光寺とともに八尾城の一郭に組み込まれていたと推定されるため、この兵火で罹災したと思われる。また、八尾荘は{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}の末期にも河内国{{jalink|守護|Q1141023}}の{{jalink|畠山氏|Q662881}}の内紛のために戦乱の地となっており、この際にも罹災したと思われる。 近世には牛頭天王社として'''天徳寺'''を{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}とする西郷村の氏神とされたが、明治初めの{{jalink|神仏判然令}}を受けて天徳寺を廃寺にして社名を「栗栖神社」に改め、明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）4月に木戸村の八幡社を合祀、西郷・木戸両村の氏神として村社に列した。明治41年4月15日に萱振村（現萱振町）の{{jalink|加津良神社}}と八尾中野（現西山本町）の八阪神社を合祀して現社名に改称したが、戦後に加津良、八阪の両神社は復祀している。 === 社殿 === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|右（北）から本殿、幣殿、拝殿]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|八尾城址の碑（左）と菅原神社（右）]] {{jalink|本殿|Q2044994}}は{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}}、明治中頃の造替。{{jalink|幣殿|Q3076699}}は梁間1間桁行3間の{{jalink|切妻造|Q13308575}}{{jalink|妻入}}、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}13年（1924年）の建築で{{jalink|拝殿|Q3125745}}に接続する。拝殿は桁行5間梁間3間の{{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}}{{jalink|平入|Q3136194}}、{{jalink|天保|Q1369045}}12年（1841年）の建築。幣殿と拝殿は{{jalink|瓦葺}}。 その他、神輿舎、社務所がある。 === 境内 === 境内社に'''鶯神社'''、'''菅原神社'''の2社がある。また本殿の東側、菅原神社の横に{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}6年（1931年）に建碑された八尾城址の{{jalink|石碑|Q178743}}がある。 === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|近鉄大阪線|Q1052545}}{{jalink|近鉄八尾駅|Q6414378|八尾駅}}から西へ約5分 === 関連項目 === * 初日山{{jalink|常光寺 (八尾市)|Q25248064|常光寺}}（{{jalink|八尾地蔵尊}}） * {{jalink|八尾天満宮}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ 公式] * {{jalink|八尾妙法寺}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * 八尾山{{jalink|智崇院}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html 公式] * {{jalink|天王神社|Q11443028}} * {{jalink|八尾 (八尾市)|Q11390778}} * {{jalink|矢作神社 (八尾市)|Q11583922}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * 式内社研究會編 『式内社調査報告』第4巻河内国、{{jalink|皇學館大學||皇學館大學出版部}}、昭和54年 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|lt=Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11545372|lt=Q11545372|WD=Q11545372}} * {{ill|Q11071125|lt=Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9122005001861 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市本町7丁目7-27', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11390855}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yao Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka01.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}Honmachi 7-7-27 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} | 別名=Gozu Tennō-sha | 例祭=October 15 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yao Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yao Shrine''' (やおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is identified with "'''Kurusu Shrine'''" in Wakae District of {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} (a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}), and its rank was former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of the former villages of Nishi-gō and Kido in Wakae District (now roughly the north side of Honmachi 5-chōme, Honmachi 6-7 chōme, and Higashi-Honmachi 3-chōme in Yao){{efn|The shrine's former address notation was Yao-shi Ōaza Nishi-gō 1064; east of the precincts is the Nishi-gō district community hall.}}. === Shrine name === In the early modern period it was called "{{jalink|Gozu Tennō-sha}}," but after the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}} it was renamed "Kurusu Shrine" in accordance with the Engishiki, then renamed again to the present name on April 15, Meiji 41 (1908). "{{jalink|Kurusu|Q136002285}}" is thought to have been the old name of the surrounding area. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Umashimaji|Q10946169|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} In the early modern period, as the name Gozu Tennō-sha indicates, {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} was worshipped as deity. Since ''{{jalink|Kawachi-shi}}'' of {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 20 (1735), however, it has been identified with the shikinaisha "Kurusu Shrine." "Kurusu" is considered connected to the ancient {{jalink|clan|Q211503}} {{jalink|Kurusu no Muraji}} who had this area as their {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, and thus the deity came to be regarded as their ancestral deity Umashimaji-no-Mikoto, as in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' (Kawachi no Kuni, section of kami-descended clans, Kurusu no Muraji). After Meiji, the deity was formally changed to Umashimaji-no-Mikoto. Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto is the deity of Hachiman Shrine of former Kido village, merged in Meiji 5. === History === The shrine site lies along {{jalink|Nagase River (Osaka)|Q11653384|Nagase River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), on slightly elevated land where a settlement likely existed from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. Though founding date is unknown, as the Ato area of Yao had Moriya no Mononobe's villa of Ato (recorded in {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}, entry for 587), Yao is deeply connected with the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} and related groups; it is considered to have begun when the Kurusu no Muraji, kin of Mononobe and based here, enshrined their ancestral deity, dating back to the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}. On April 12 of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 4 ({{jalink|862|Q24993}}), it was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and on Nov 11 that year was made an official shrine (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engi system}}, it was listed as a government-offering small shrine. Details after the medieval period are unclear, but the surrounding area was called Yao-no-shō and this shrine is thought to have been revered as its central shrine. In the Nanboku-chō period, monk Kenkō of nearby {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (called "Yao Bettō") joined the Southern Court and held {{jalink|Yao Castle|Q11390786}}, but after the Northern Court took the castle following the Battle of Minatogawa, Southern forces attacked in {{jalink|Engen|Q1194159}} 2 (1337), bringing war fire. This shrine may then have served as Jōkō-ji's guardian and likely suffered damage as part of the castle complex. Yao-no-shō was again a war zone in the late Muromachi period during internal conflict of the {{jalink|Hatakeyama clan|Q662881}}, and likely suffered again. In early modern times it was the ujigami of Nishi-gō village as Gozu Tennō-sha with Tendoku-ji as its {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}. Following early Meiji separation orders, Tendoku-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Kurusu Shrine; in April Meiji 5 (1872) Hachiman Shrine of Kido village was merged, and it became village shrine of both villages. On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{jalink|honden|Q2044994}} is {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}{{jalink|Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{jalink|Yao Tenmangū}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{jalink|Yao Myōhō-ji}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{jalink|Chisū-in}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{jalink|Tennō Shrine|Q11443028}} * {{jalink|Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778}} * {{jalink|Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{jalink|Kogakkan University Press}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9122005001861 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市本町7丁目7-27', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11390855}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yao Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka01.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}Honmachi 7-7-27 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} | 別名=Gozu Tennō-sha | 例祭=October 15 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yao Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yao Shrine''' (やおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is identified with "'''Kurusu Shrine'''" in Wakae District of {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} (a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}), and its rank was former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of the former villages of Nishi-gō and Kido in Wakae District (now roughly the north side of Honmachi 5-chōme, Honmachi 6-7 chōme, and Higashi-Honmachi 3-chōme in Yao){{efn|The shrine's former address notation was Yao-shi Ōaza Nishi-gō 1064; east of the precincts is the Nishi-gō district community hall.}}. === Shrine name === In the early modern period it was called "{{jalink|Gozu Tennō-sha}}," but after the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}} it was renamed "Kurusu Shrine" in accordance with the Engishiki, then renamed again to the present name on April 15, Meiji 41 (1908). "{{jalink|Kurusu|Q136002285}}" is thought to have been the old name of the surrounding area. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Umashimaji|Q10946169|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} In the early modern period, as the name Gozu Tennō-sha indicates, {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} was worshipped as deity. Since ''{{jalink|Kawachi-shi}}'' of {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 20 (1735), however, it has been identified with the shikinaisha "Kurusu Shrine." "Kurusu" is considered connected to the ancient {{jalink|clan|Q211503}} {{jalink|Kurusu no Muraji}} who had this area as their {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, and thus the deity came to be regarded as their ancestral deity Umashimaji-no-Mikoto, as in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' (Kawachi no Kuni, section of kami-descended clans, Kurusu no Muraji). After Meiji, the deity was formally changed to Umashimaji-no-Mikoto. Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto is the deity of Hachiman Shrine of former Kido village, merged in Meiji 5. === History === The shrine site lies along {{jalink|Nagase River (Osaka)|Q11653384|Nagase River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), on slightly elevated land where a settlement likely existed from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. Though founding date is unknown, as the Ato area of Yao had Moriya no Mononobe's villa of Ato (recorded in {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}, entry for 587), Yao is deeply connected with the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} and related groups; it is considered to have begun when the Kurusu no Muraji, kin of Mononobe and based here, enshrined their ancestral deity, dating back to the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}. On April 12 of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 4 ({{jalink|862|Q24993}}), it was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and on Nov 11 that year was made an official shrine (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engi system}}, it was listed as a government-offering small shrine. Details after the medieval period are unclear, but the surrounding area was called Yao-no-shō and this shrine is thought to have been revered as its central shrine. In the Nanboku-chō period, monk Kenkō of nearby {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (called "Yao Bettō") joined the Southern Court and held {{jalink|Yao Castle|Q11390786}}, but after the Northern Court took the castle following the Battle of Minatogawa, Southern forces attacked in {{jalink|Engen|Q1194159}} 2 (1337), bringing war fire. This shrine may then have served as Jōkō-ji's guardian and likely suffered damage as part of the castle complex. Yao-no-shō was again a war zone in the late Muromachi period during internal conflict of the {{jalink|Hatakeyama clan|Q662881}}, and likely suffered again. In early modern times it was the ujigami of Nishi-gō village as Gozu Tennō-sha with Tendoku-ji as its {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}. Following early Meiji separation orders, Tendoku-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Kurusu Shrine; in April Meiji 5 (1872) Hachiman Shrine of Kido village was merged, and it became village shrine of both villages. On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{jalink|honden|Q2044994}} is {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}{{jalink|Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{jalink|Yao Tenmangū}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{jalink|Yao Myōhō-ji}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{jalink|Chisū-in}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{jalink|Tennō Shrine|Q11443028}} * {{jalink|Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778}} * {{jalink|Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{jalink|Kogakkan University Press}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}{{jalink|Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{jalink|Yao Tenmangū}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{jalink|Yao Myōhō-ji}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{jalink|Chisū-in}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{jalink|Tennō Shrine|Q11443028}} * {{jalink|Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778}} * {{jalink|Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{jalink|Kogakkan University Press}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9122005001861 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市本町7丁目7-27', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11390855}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yao Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka01.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}Honmachi 7-7-27 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} | 別名=Gozu Tennō-sha | 例祭=October 15 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yao Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yao Shrine''' (やおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is identified with "'''Kurusu Shrine'''" in Wakae District of {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} (a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}), and its rank was former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of the former villages of Nishi-gō and Kido in Wakae District (now roughly the north side of Honmachi 5-chōme, Honmachi 6-7 chōme, and Higashi-Honmachi 3-chōme in Yao){{efn|The shrine's former address notation was Yao-shi Ōaza Nishi-gō 1064; east of the precincts is the Nishi-gō district community hall.}}. === Shrine name === In the early modern period it was called "{{jalink|Gozu Tennō-sha}}," but after the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}} it was renamed "Kurusu Shrine" in accordance with the Engishiki, then renamed again to the present name on April 15, Meiji 41 (1908). "{{jalink|Kurusu|Q136002285}}" is thought to have been the old name of the surrounding area. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Umashimaji|Q10946169|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} In the early modern period, as the name Gozu Tennō-sha indicates, {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} was worshipped as deity. Since ''{{jalink|Kawachi-shi}}'' of {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 20 (1735), however, it has been identified with the shikinaisha "Kurusu Shrine." "Kurusu" is considered connected to the ancient {{jalink|clan|Q211503}} {{jalink|Kurusu no Muraji}} who had this area as their {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, and thus the deity came to be regarded as their ancestral deity Umashimaji-no-Mikoto, as in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' (Kawachi no Kuni, section of kami-descended clans, Kurusu no Muraji). After Meiji, the deity was formally changed to Umashimaji-no-Mikoto. Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto is the deity of Hachiman Shrine of former Kido village, merged in Meiji 5. === History === The shrine site lies along {{jalink|Nagase River (Osaka)|Q11653384|Nagase River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), on slightly elevated land where a settlement likely existed from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. Though founding date is unknown, as the Ato area of Yao had Moriya no Mononobe's villa of Ato (recorded in {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}, entry for 587), Yao is deeply connected with the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} and related groups; it is considered to have begun when the Kurusu no Muraji, kin of Mononobe and based here, enshrined their ancestral deity, dating back to the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}. On April 12 of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 4 ({{jalink|862|Q24993}}), it was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and on Nov 11 that year was made an official shrine (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engi system}}, it was listed as a government-offering small shrine. Details after the medieval period are unclear, but the surrounding area was called Yao-no-shō and this shrine is thought to have been revered as its central shrine. In the Nanboku-chō period, monk Kenkō of nearby {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (called "Yao Bettō") joined the Southern Court and held {{jalink|Yao Castle|Q11390786}}, but after the Northern Court took the castle following the Battle of Minatogawa, Southern forces attacked in {{jalink|Engen|Q1194159}} 2 (1337), bringing war fire. This shrine may then have served as Jōkō-ji's guardian and likely suffered damage as part of the castle complex. Yao-no-shō was again a war zone in the late Muromachi period during internal conflict of the {{jalink|Hatakeyama clan|Q662881}}, and likely suffered again. In early modern times it was the ujigami of Nishi-gō village as Gozu Tennō-sha with Tendoku-ji as its {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}. Following early Meiji separation orders, Tendoku-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Kurusu Shrine; in April Meiji 5 (1872) Hachiman Shrine of Kido village was merged, and it became village shrine of both villages. On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{jalink|honden|Q2044994}} is {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}{{jalink|Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{jalink|Yao Tenmangū}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{jalink|Yao Myōhō-ji}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{jalink|Chisū-in}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{jalink|Tennō Shrine|Q11443028}} * {{jalink|Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778}} * {{jalink|Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{jalink|Kogakkan University Press}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is identified with "'''Kurusu Shrine'''" in Wakae District of {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} (a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}), and its rank was former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of the former villages of Nishi-gō and Kido in Wakae District (now roughly the north side of Honmachi 5-chōme, Honmachi 6-7 chōme, and Higashi-Honmachi 3-chōme in Yao){{efn|The shrine's former address notation was Yao-shi Ōaza Nishi-gō 1064; east of the precincts is the Nishi-gō district community hall.}}. === Shrine name === In the early modern period it was called "{{jalink|Gozu Tennō-sha}}," but after the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}} it was renamed "Kurusu Shrine" in accordance with the Engishiki, then renamed again to the present name on April 15, Meiji 41 (1908). "{{jalink|Kurusu|Q136002285}}" is thought to have been the old name of the surrounding area. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Umashimaji|Q10946169|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} In the early modern period, as the name Gozu Tennō-sha indicates, {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} was worshipped as deity. Since ''{{jalink|Kawachi-shi}}'' of {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 20 (1735), however, it has been identified with the shikinaisha "Kurusu Shrine." "Kurusu" is considered connected to the ancient {{jalink|clan|Q211503}} {{jalink|Kurusu no Muraji}} who had this area as their {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, and thus the deity came to be regarded as their ancestral deity Umashimaji-no-Mikoto, as in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' (Kawachi no Kuni, section of kami-descended clans, Kurusu no Muraji). After Meiji, the deity was formally changed to Umashimaji-no-Mikoto. Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto is the deity of Hachiman Shrine of former Kido village, merged in Meiji 5. === History === The shrine site lies along {{jalink|Nagase River (Osaka)|Q11653384|Nagase River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), on slightly elevated land where a settlement likely existed from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. Though founding date is unknown, as the Ato area of Yao had Moriya no Mononobe's villa of Ato (recorded in {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}, entry for 587), Yao is deeply connected with the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} and related groups; it is considered to have begun when the Kurusu no Muraji, kin of Mononobe and based here, enshrined their ancestral deity, dating back to the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}. On April 12 of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 4 ({{jalink|862|Q24993}}), it was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and on Nov 11 that year was made an official shrine (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engi system}}, it was listed as a government-offering small shrine. Details after the medieval period are unclear, but the surrounding area was called Yao-no-shō and this shrine is thought to have been revered as its central shrine. In the Nanboku-chō period, monk Kenkō of nearby {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (called "Yao Bettō") joined the Southern Court and held {{jalink|Yao Castle|Q11390786}}, but after the Northern Court took the castle following the Battle of Minatogawa, Southern forces attacked in {{jalink|Engen|Q1194159}} 2 (1337), bringing war fire. This shrine may then have served as Jōkō-ji's guardian and likely suffered damage as part of the castle complex. Yao-no-shō was again a war zone in the late Muromachi period during internal conflict of the {{jalink|Hatakeyama clan|Q662881}}, and likely suffered again. In early modern times it was the ujigami of Nishi-gō village as Gozu Tennō-sha with Tendoku-ji as its {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}. Following early Meiji separation orders, Tendoku-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Kurusu Shrine; in April Meiji 5 (1872) Hachiman Shrine of Kido village was merged, and it became village shrine of both villages. On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{jalink|honden|Q2044994}} is {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}{{jalink|Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{jalink|Yao Tenmangū}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{jalink|Yao Myōhō-ji}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{jalink|Chisū-in}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{jalink|Tennō Shrine|Q11443028}} * {{jalink|Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778}} * {{jalink|Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{jalink|Kogakkan University Press}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9122005001861 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市本町7丁目7-27', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11390855}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yao Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka01.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}Honmachi 7-7-27 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) / former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} | 別名=Gozu Tennō-sha | 例祭=October 15 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yao Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yao Shrine''' (やおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is identified with "'''Kurusu Shrine'''" in Wakae District of {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}} in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} (a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}), and its rank was former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of the former villages of Nishi-gō and Kido in Wakae District (now roughly the north side of Honmachi 5-chōme, Honmachi 6-7 chōme, and Higashi-Honmachi 3-chōme in Yao){{efn|The shrine's former address notation was Yao-shi Ōaza Nishi-gō 1064; east of the precincts is the Nishi-gō district community hall.}}. === Shrine name === In the early modern period it was called "{{jalink|Gozu Tennō-sha}}," but after the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}} it was renamed "Kurusu Shrine" in accordance with the Engishiki, then renamed again to the present name on April 15, Meiji 41 (1908). "{{jalink|Kurusu|Q136002285}}" is thought to have been the old name of the surrounding area. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Umashimaji|Q10946169|Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}} In the early modern period, as the name Gozu Tennō-sha indicates, {{jalink|Gozu Tennō|Q11570247}} was worshipped as deity. Since ''{{jalink|Kawachi-shi}}'' of {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 20 (1735), however, it has been identified with the shikinaisha "Kurusu Shrine." "Kurusu" is considered connected to the ancient {{jalink|clan|Q211503}} {{jalink|Kurusu no Muraji}} who had this area as their {{jalink|honkan|Q846706}}, and thus the deity came to be regarded as their ancestral deity Umashimaji-no-Mikoto, as in ''{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'' (Kawachi no Kuni, section of kami-descended clans, Kurusu no Muraji). After Meiji, the deity was formally changed to Umashimaji-no-Mikoto. Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto is the deity of Hachiman Shrine of former Kido village, merged in Meiji 5. === History === The shrine site lies along {{jalink|Nagase River (Osaka)|Q11653384|Nagase River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), on slightly elevated land where a settlement likely existed from the {{jalink|Yayoi period|Q207858}}''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. Though founding date is unknown, as the Ato area of Yao had Moriya no Mononobe's villa of Ato (recorded in {{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}, entry for 587), Yao is deeply connected with the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}} and related groups; it is considered to have begun when the Kurusu no Muraji, kin of Mononobe and based here, enshrined their ancestral deity, dating back to the {{jalink|Kofun period|Q459939}}. On April 12 of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 4 ({{jalink|862|Q24993}}), it was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and on Nov 11 that year was made an official shrine (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}''). In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engi system}}, it was listed as a government-offering small shrine. Details after the medieval period are unclear, but the surrounding area was called Yao-no-shō and this shrine is thought to have been revered as its central shrine. In the Nanboku-chō period, monk Kenkō of nearby {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (called "Yao Bettō") joined the Southern Court and held {{jalink|Yao Castle|Q11390786}}, but after the Northern Court took the castle following the Battle of Minatogawa, Southern forces attacked in {{jalink|Engen|Q1194159}} 2 (1337), bringing war fire. This shrine may then have served as Jōkō-ji's guardian and likely suffered damage as part of the castle complex. Yao-no-shō was again a war zone in the late Muromachi period during internal conflict of the {{jalink|Hatakeyama clan|Q662881}}, and likely suffered again. In early modern times it was the ujigami of Nishi-gō village as Gozu Tennō-sha with Tendoku-ji as its {{jalink|jingūji|Q1074971}}. Following early Meiji separation orders, Tendoku-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Kurusu Shrine; in April Meiji 5 (1872) Hachiman Shrine of Kido village was merged, and it became village shrine of both villages. On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{jalink|honden|Q2044994}} is {{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{jalink|heiden|Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{jalink|kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{jalink|Taishō|Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{jalink|haiden|Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{jalink|Tenpō|Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{jalink|Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545}}{{jalink|Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{jalink|Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{jalink|Yao Tenmangū}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{jalink|Yao Myōhō-ji}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{jalink|Chisū-in}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{jalink|Tennō Shrine|Q11443028}} * {{jalink|Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778}} * {{jalink|Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{jalink|Kogakkan University Press}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} is {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545}}{{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|WD=Q6414378|lt=Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{ill|lt=Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|WD=Q25248064|lt=Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{ill||lt=Yao Tenmangū|UNKNOWN}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{ill||lt=Yao Myōhō-ji|UNKNOWN}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{ill||lt=Chisū-in|UNKNOWN}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{ill|lt=Tennō Shrine|Q11443028|WD=Q11443028}} * {{ill|lt=Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778|WD=Q11390778}} * {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{ill||lt=Kogakkan University Press|UNKNOWN}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9122005001861 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市本町7丁目7-27', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11390855}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yao Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka01.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Honmachi 7-7-27 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} | 別名=Gozu Tennō-sha | 例祭=October 15 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yao Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yao Shrine''' (やおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is identified with "'''Kurusu Shrine'''" in Wakae District of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}), and its rank was former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the former villages of Nishi-gō and Kido in Wakae District (now roughly the north side of Honmachi 5-chōme, Honmachi 6-7 chōme, and Higashi-Honmachi 3-chōme in Yao){{efn|The shrine's former address notation was Yao-shi Ōaza Nishi-gō 1064; east of the precincts is the Nishi-gō district community hall.}}. === Shrine name === In the early modern period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gozu Tennō-sha|UNKNOWN}}," but after the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}} it was renamed "Kurusu Shrine" in accordance with the Engishiki, then renamed again to the present name on April 15, Meiji 41 (1908). "{{ill|lt=Kurusu|Q136002285|WD=Q136002285}}" is thought to have been the old name of the surrounding area. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} In the early modern period, as the name Gozu Tennō-sha indicates, {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} was worshipped as deity. Since ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi-shi|UNKNOWN}}'' of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 20 (1735), however, it has been identified with the shikinaisha "Kurusu Shrine." "Kurusu" is considered connected to the ancient {{ill|lt=clan|Q211503|WD=Q211503}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kurusu no Muraji|UNKNOWN}} who had this area as their {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706}}, and thus the deity came to be regarded as their ancestral deity Umashimaji-no-Mikoto, as in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' (Kawachi no Kuni, section of kami-descended clans, Kurusu no Muraji). After Meiji, the deity was formally changed to Umashimaji-no-Mikoto. Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto is the deity of Hachiman Shrine of former Kido village, merged in Meiji 5. === History === The shrine site lies along {{ill|lt=Nagase River (Osaka)|Q11653384|WD=Q11653384|lt=Nagase River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527}}), on slightly elevated land where a settlement likely existed from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}}''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. Though founding date is unknown, as the Ato area of Yao had Moriya no Mononobe's villa of Ato (recorded in {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}, entry for 587), Yao is deeply connected with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} and related groups; it is considered to have begun when the Kurusu no Muraji, kin of Mononobe and based here, enshrined their ancestral deity, dating back to the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. On April 12 of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 4 ({{ill|lt=862|Q24993|WD=Q24993}}), it was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and on Nov 11 that year was made an official shrine (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system}}, it was listed as a government-offering small shrine. Details after the medieval period are unclear, but the surrounding area was called Yao-no-shō and this shrine is thought to have been revered as its central shrine. In the Nanboku-chō period, monk Kenkō of nearby {{ill|lt=Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|WD=Q25248064|lt=Jōkō-ji}} (called "Yao Bettō") joined the Southern Court and held {{ill|lt=Yao Castle|Q11390786|WD=Q11390786}}, but after the Northern Court took the castle following the Battle of Minatogawa, Southern forces attacked in {{ill|lt=Engen|Q1194159|WD=Q1194159}} 2 (1337), bringing war fire. This shrine may then have served as Jōkō-ji's guardian and likely suffered damage as part of the castle complex. Yao-no-shō was again a war zone in the late Muromachi period during internal conflict of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881}}, and likely suffered again. In early modern times it was the ujigami of Nishi-gō village as Gozu Tennō-sha with Tendoku-ji as its {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. Following early Meiji separation orders, Tendoku-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Kurusu Shrine; in April Meiji 5 (1872) Hachiman Shrine of Kido village was merged, and it became village shrine of both villages. On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} is {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545}}{{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|WD=Q6414378|lt=Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{ill|lt=Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|WD=Q25248064|lt=Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yao Tenmangū|UNKNOWN}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yao Myōhō-ji|UNKNOWN}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chisū-in|UNKNOWN}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{ill|lt=Tennō Shrine|Q11443028|WD=Q11443028}} * {{ill|lt=Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778|WD=Q11390778}} * {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press|UNKNOWN}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11545372|lt=Q11545372|WD=Q11545372}} * {{ill|Q11071125|lt=Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9122005001861 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府八尾市本町7丁目7-27', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八尾神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11390855}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yao Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka01.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Honmachi 7-7-27 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} | 別名=Gozu Tennō-sha | 例祭=October 15 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yao Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yao Shrine''' (やおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is identified with "'''Kurusu Shrine'''" in Wakae District of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}), and its rank was former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the former villages of Nishi-gō and Kido in Wakae District (now roughly the north side of Honmachi 5-chōme, Honmachi 6-7 chōme, and Higashi-Honmachi 3-chōme in Yao){{efn|The shrine's former address notation was Yao-shi Ōaza Nishi-gō 1064; east of the precincts is the Nishi-gō district community hall.}}. === Shrine name === In the early modern period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gozu Tennō-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but after the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}} it was renamed "Kurusu Shrine" in accordance with the Engishiki, then renamed again to the present name on April 15, Meiji 41 (1908). "{{ill|lt=Kurusu|Q136002285|WD=Q136002285}}" is thought to have been the old name of the surrounding area. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} In the early modern period, as the name Gozu Tennō-sha indicates, {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} was worshipped as deity. Since ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi-shi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 20 (1735), however, it has been identified with the shikinaisha "Kurusu Shrine." "Kurusu" is considered connected to the ancient {{ill|lt=clan|Q211503|WD=Q211503}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kurusu no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} who had this area as their {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706}}, and thus the deity came to be regarded as their ancestral deity Umashimaji-no-Mikoto, as in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' (Kawachi no Kuni, section of kami-descended clans, Kurusu no Muraji). After Meiji, the deity was formally changed to Umashimaji-no-Mikoto. Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto is the deity of Hachiman Shrine of former Kido village, merged in Meiji 5. === History === The shrine site lies along {{ill|lt=Nagase River (Osaka)|Q11653384|WD=Q11653384|lt=Nagase River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527}}), on slightly elevated land where a settlement likely existed from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}}''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. Though founding date is unknown, as the Ato area of Yao had Moriya no Mononobe's villa of Ato (recorded in {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}, entry for 587), Yao is deeply connected with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} and related groups; it is considered to have begun when the Kurusu no Muraji, kin of Mononobe and based here, enshrined their ancestral deity, dating back to the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. On April 12 of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 4 ({{ill|lt=862|Q24993|WD=Q24993}}), it was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and on Nov 11 that year was made an official shrine (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system}}, it was listed as a government-offering small shrine. Details after the medieval period are unclear, but the surrounding area was called Yao-no-shō and this shrine is thought to have been revered as its central shrine. In the Nanboku-chō period, monk Kenkō of nearby {{ill|lt=Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|WD=Q25248064|lt=Jōkō-ji}} (called "Yao Bettō") joined the Southern Court and held {{ill|lt=Yao Castle|Q11390786|WD=Q11390786}}, but after the Northern Court took the castle following the Battle of Minatogawa, Southern forces attacked in {{ill|lt=Engen|Q1194159|WD=Q1194159}} 2 (1337), bringing war fire. This shrine may then have served as Jōkō-ji's guardian and likely suffered damage as part of the castle complex. Yao-no-shō was again a war zone in the late Muromachi period during internal conflict of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881}}, and likely suffered again. In early modern times it was the ujigami of Nishi-gō village as Gozu Tennō-sha with Tendoku-ji as its {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. Following early Meiji separation orders, Tendoku-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Kurusu Shrine; in April Meiji 5 (1872) Hachiman Shrine of Kido village was merged, and it became village shrine of both villages. On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} is {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545}}{{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|WD=Q6414378|lt=Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{ill|lt=Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|WD=Q25248064|lt=Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yao Tenmangū|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yao Myōhō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chisū-in|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{ill|lt=Tennō Shrine|Q11443028|WD=Q11443028}} * {{ill|lt=Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778|WD=Q11390778}} * {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545}}{{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|WD=Q6414378|lt=Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{ill|lt=Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|WD=Q25248064|lt=Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yao Tenmangū|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yao Myōhō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chisū-in|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{ill|lt=Tennō Shrine|Q11443028|WD=Q11443028}} * {{ill|lt=Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778|WD=Q11390778}} * {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Yao Shrine | 画像=[[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka01.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Honmachi 7-7-27 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} | 別名=Gozu Tennō-sha | 例祭=October 15 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Yao Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Yao Shrine''' (やおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is identified with "'''Kurusu Shrine'''" in Wakae District of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} (a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}), and its rank was former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the former villages of Nishi-gō and Kido in Wakae District (now roughly the north side of Honmachi 5-chōme, Honmachi 6-7 chōme, and Higashi-Honmachi 3-chōme in Yao){{efn|The shrine's former address notation was Yao-shi Ōaza Nishi-gō 1064; east of the precincts is the Nishi-gō district community hall.}}. === Shrine name === In the early modern period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gozu Tennō-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but after the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}} it was renamed "Kurusu Shrine" in accordance with the Engishiki, then renamed again to the present name on April 15, Meiji 41 (1908). "{{ill|lt=Kurusu|Q136002285|WD=Q136002285}}" is thought to have been the old name of the surrounding area. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} In the early modern period, as the name Gozu Tennō-sha indicates, {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247}} was worshipped as deity. Since ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi-shi|1=UNKNOWN}}'' of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 20 (1735), however, it has been identified with the shikinaisha "Kurusu Shrine." "Kurusu" is considered connected to the ancient {{ill|lt=clan|Q211503|WD=Q211503}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kurusu no Muraji|1=UNKNOWN}} who had this area as their {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706}}, and thus the deity came to be regarded as their ancestral deity Umashimaji-no-Mikoto, as in ''{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'' (Kawachi no Kuni, section of kami-descended clans, Kurusu no Muraji). After Meiji, the deity was formally changed to Umashimaji-no-Mikoto. Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto is the deity of Hachiman Shrine of former Kido village, merged in Meiji 5. === History === The shrine site lies along {{ill|lt=Nagase River (Osaka)|Q11653384|WD=Q11653384|lt=Nagase River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527}}), on slightly elevated land where a settlement likely existed from the {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|WD=Q207858}}''Shikinaisha Survey Report''.. Though founding date is unknown, as the Ato area of Yao had Moriya no Mononobe's villa of Ato (recorded in {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}, entry for 587), Yao is deeply connected with the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}} and related groups; it is considered to have begun when the Kurusu no Muraji, kin of Mononobe and based here, enshrined their ancestral deity, dating back to the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939}}. On April 12 of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 4 ({{ill|lt=862|Q24993|WD=Q24993}}), it was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower, and on Nov 11 that year was made an official shrine (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}''). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system}}, it was listed as a government-offering small shrine. Details after the medieval period are unclear, but the surrounding area was called Yao-no-shō and this shrine is thought to have been revered as its central shrine. In the Nanboku-chō period, monk Kenkō of nearby {{ill|lt=Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|WD=Q25248064|lt=Jōkō-ji}} (called "Yao Bettō") joined the Southern Court and held {{ill|lt=Yao Castle|Q11390786|WD=Q11390786}}, but after the Northern Court took the castle following the Battle of Minatogawa, Southern forces attacked in {{ill|lt=Engen|Q1194159|WD=Q1194159}} 2 (1337), bringing war fire. This shrine may then have served as Jōkō-ji's guardian and likely suffered damage as part of the castle complex. Yao-no-shō was again a war zone in the late Muromachi period during internal conflict of the {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama clan|Q662881|WD=Q662881}}, and likely suffered again. In early modern times it was the ujigami of Nishi-gō village as Gozu Tennō-sha with Tendoku-ji as its {{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}. Following early Meiji separation orders, Tendoku-ji was abolished and the shrine renamed Kurusu Shrine; in April Meiji 5 (1872) Hachiman Shrine of Kido village was merged, and it became village shrine of both villages. On April 15, Meiji 41, Katsura Shrine (Kaya-buri village) and Yasaka Shrine (Yao Nakano) were merged and the shrine took its current name; after the war, both shrines were restored separately. === Shrine buildings === [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka04.JPG|thumb|right|200px|From right (north): honden, heiden, haiden]] [[File:Yao Jinja, Yao, Osaka03.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Yao Castle site monument (left) and Sugawara Shrine (right)]] The {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} is {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}}, rebuilt in mid-Meiji. The {{ill|lt=heiden|Q3076699|WD=Q3076699}} is a 1-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=kirizuma-zukuri|Q13308575|WD=Q13308575}} with gable entrance, built in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 13 (1924), connected to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745}}. The haiden is a 5-by-3 bay {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} hira-iri building from {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045}} 12 (1841). The heiden and haiden are tile-roofed. There are also a mikoshi storehouse and shrine office. === Precincts === There are two precinct shrines: Uguisu Shrine and Sugawara Shrine. On the east side of the honden, beside Sugawara Shrine, stands a stone monument to Yao Castle erected in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 6 (1931). === Access === * About 5 minutes west from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|Q1052545|WD=Q1052545}}{{ill|lt=Kintetsu Yao Station|Q6414378|WD=Q6414378|lt=Yao Station}} === Related items === * Hatsuhiyama {{ill|lt=Jōkō-ji (Yao)|Q25248064|WD=Q25248064|lt=Jōkō-ji}} (Yao Jizōson) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yao Tenmangū|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://www.yao-tenmangu.com/ Official] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yao Myōhō-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://yaokami.jp/1277253/] * Yaosan {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chisū-in|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://tisouin.org/enkaku.html Official] * {{ill|lt=Tennō Shrine|Q11443028|WD=Q11443028}} * {{ill|lt=Yao (Yao city)|Q11390778|WD=Q11390778}} * {{ill|lt=Yahagi Shrine (Yao)|Q11583922|WD=Q11583922}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === {{Commons|Category:Yao-jinja}} * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol.4 Kawachi Province, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University Press|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1979 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やおしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yao Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Yao Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:栗栖氏]] [[Category:若江郡|社 やおしんしや]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八尾 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
